goal: Land plugin manifests + smoke verifications for dependency-cruiser (G4 Architecture, P0 JS/TS, master-plan §4.4 line 328) and Zoekt (G2 Search, P1, master-plan §4.2 line 300) — two CLI/external-service plugins that follow the WO-0051 ripgrep template (declarative manifest + parse-only test + verify-script smoke) since neither speaks stdio JSON-RPC. dep-cruiser detects circular dependency on a tmpdir-fabricated TS module pair (fixture is read-only); Zoekt builds a trigram index over a tmpdir corpus and asserts query correctness vs ripgrep on warm index. This is the explicit emission recommended by WO-0085 §lessons-learned 'For planner' bullet 3.
plan_summary: Two-feature CLI/external-service plugin-manifest landing WO mirroring WO-0051 (ripgrep manifest+smoke) extended to two plugins per WO-0044 (ast-grep+probe pair) bundle shape. F14 (dependency-cruiser): NEW plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh + ONE new sync test `dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses` inside the existing `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs (parse-only since dep-cruiser is a CLI not MCP — same shape as ripgrep_manifest_parses in plugin_manifests.rs). F15 (Zoekt): NEW plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml + scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh + replacement scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh + ONE new sync test `zoekt_manifest_parses` inside the existing `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` block in crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs (parse-only since Zoekt is HTTP-API/server not stdio MCP — same shape as ripgrep_manifest_parses). Both manifests use stdio sentinel `[transport]` per the WO-0051 DEC-0009 declarative-metadata pattern (the daemon does not invoke these transports — they are declarative metadata so the planner can enumerate dep-cruiser as `architecture.dependency` and Zoekt as `search.text` providers). Pre-baked M1/M2 mutation contracts per the 41-WO-deep standing protocol. Tier-1 install paths: `npm install -g dependency-cruiser@<PINNED-SEMVER>` (Tier-1 for F14) AND `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt-index@<PINNED-COMMIT>` + `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt@<PINNED-COMMIT>` (Tier-1 for F15). F14 verify-script smoke fabricates a circular-dep TS module pair in a `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy of tests/fixtures/typescript-project (since the fixture itself is a clean DAG — verified at planner time via `grep -n import tests/fixtures/typescript-project/src/*.ts` showing types.ts → no imports, filter-engine/repository/task-manager → linear DAG without cycle). F15 verify-script smoke builds a Zoekt trigram index over a tmpdir copy of fixture projects, runs an identical query through both Zoekt and ripgrep, asserts the matched-path sets are equivalent (correctness invariant — load-bearing), AND prints both wall-clocks for the verifier log; the wall-clock comparison is asserted with a loose 50ms tolerance to absorb small-corpus noise (Zoekt's `faster than ripgrep on warm index` per master-plan §4.2 spec — passes trivially on representative corpora and tolerates fixture-scale noise). Both F14 and F15 have `attempts=0` (clean first attempt expected per the 41-WO-deep mature template).
scope_in: 1. Create NEW `plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` mirroring the structure of `plugins/search/ripgrep/plugin.toml` (the closest sibling: in-process / CLI tool with declarative-sentinel transport per DEC-0009). Header comment block (15-25 lines) cites: master-plan §4.4 line 328 (dependency-cruiser P0 JS/TS), master-plan §14.1 (architecture category), DEC-0009 (CLI-tool / in-process tools use a declarative transport sentinel — the daemon does not spawn this transport because dep-cruiser does not speak MCP/JSON-RPC; the manifest is metadata so the planner can enumerate dep-cruiser as an `architecture.dependency` provider). `[plugin]` table: `name = "dependency-cruiser"`, `version = "<PINNED-SEMVER>"` (executor pins to a frozen semver release tag captured at WO-execution time from `curl -sL https://registry.npmjs.org/dependency-cruiser | jq -r '."dist-tags".latest'` — bare `latest` / `next` / `main` / `master` / `develop` floating refs are FORBIDDEN per master-plan §13 + .claude/rules/commit-style.md), `description = "JS/TS dependency validation; rule-based circular-dep + module-rule violations; CLI tool, in-process per DEC-0009."`. `[capabilities]` table: `provides = ["architecture.dependency"]` (one capability — dep-cruiser is JS/TS architecture/dependency analysis, NOT general-purpose AST), `languages = ["javascript", "typescript"]` (per master-plan §4.4 row priority `P0 (JS/TS)`). `[transport]` table: `type = "stdio"` (declarative sentinel), `command = "depcruise"`, `args = ["--version"]` (sentinel — the daemon's DEC-0009 in-process path bypasses this transport; if any future code path attempts to spawn this transport as an MCP server, the `depcruise --version` exit would correctly fail health-check, which is the desired behavior — dep-cruiser is NOT an MCP server). `[lifecycle]` table: `hot_cold = false`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 0` (per-query spawn-and-exit; no resident process). The `transport.command` field MUST be the bare binary name `"depcruise"` (not `"dependency-cruiser"`, not an absolute path) — `depcruise` is the npm package's exposed binary name per the dependency-cruiser README `npm install -g dependency-cruiser` → produces `depcruise` on PATH.

2. Create NEW `plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` mirroring the structure of `plugins/search/ripgrep/plugin.toml`. Header comment block (15-25 lines) cites: master-plan §4.2 line 300 (Zoekt P1 — Trigram-indexed search HTTP API), master-plan §14.1 (search category), master-plan §17 line 1675 (`search/ (probe/, ripgrep/, zoekt/, codedb/)` — confirms placement under `plugins/search/zoekt/`), DEC-0009 (external-service tools use a declarative transport sentinel — the daemon does not spawn Zoekt as a stdio MCP server; the manifest is metadata so the planner can enumerate Zoekt as a `search.text` provider). `[plugin]` table: `name = "zoekt"`, `version = "<PINNED-COMMIT-OR-SEMVER>"` (executor pins to a frozen 7+ char commit-SHA OR a semver release tag from https://github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/releases — bare branch/tag refs `main` / `master` / `latest` are FORBIDDEN), `description = "Trigram-indexed search for multi-repo scale; HTTP API per master-plan §4.2; in-process per DEC-0009 (manifest is metadata)."`. `[capabilities]` table: `provides = ["search.text"]` (one capability — Zoekt is text/trigram search, complementing ripgrep at multi-repo scale per master-plan §4.2), `languages = []` (Zoekt is language-agnostic trigram search; empty list signals 'all languages' per the existing CapabilitiesSection.languages semantics at crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — same convention as ripgrep). `[transport]` table: `type = "stdio"` (declarative sentinel — Zoekt is actually HTTP API per master-plan §4.2 but the manifest schema requires a `[transport]` section; the in-process / external-service short-circuit per DEC-0009 mirrors the ripgrep precedent exactly), `command = "zoekt"`, `args = ["--help"]` (sentinel — `zoekt --help` exits without speaking JSON-RPC, which is the desired behavior; the daemon does not attempt to spawn this transport as an MCP server). `[lifecycle]` table: `hot_cold = false`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 0` (Zoekt's webserver/index processes are managed externally per master-plan §4.2; from UCIL's planner's perspective, queries are per-call). Document the Zoekt-is-actually-HTTP-API divergence in the manifest header comment (1-2 lines): the manifest's `[transport]` section is a schema-required declarative sentinel; the runtime invocation pathway is via `zoekt` CLI binary (which queries Zoekt's HTTP webserver OR an in-process trigram index via the same `zoekt` binary), NOT via stdio JSON-RPC.

3. Create NEW `scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh` mirroring `scripts/devtools/install-ripgrep.sh` shape: `#!/usr/bin/env bash` + `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_DEPCRUISE_VERSION="<X.Y.Z>"` constant captured from npm at WO-execution time, detect `command -v depcruise` (print version + exit 0 if present), otherwise print install hints (`npm install -g dependency-cruiser@${PINNED_DEPCRUISE_VERSION}` is the canonical Tier-1 install — global npm install gives the `depcruise` binary on PATH; alternative hint: `npx -y dependency-cruiser@${PINNED_DEPCRUISE_VERSION}` for ephemeral one-shot use) and exit 0 (no auto-execute — informational scaffolding, same contract as install-ripgrep.sh / install-ast-grep.sh). Header comment block (8-12 lines) cites WO-0086 + P3-W10-F14 + master-plan §4.4 line 328. `chmod +x` so the script is executable. The pinned version MUST be a frozen semver — confirm `! grep -qE '"(latest|next|main|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh` returns empty.

4. Create NEW `scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh` mirroring `scripts/devtools/install-ripgrep.sh` shape: `#!/usr/bin/env bash` + `set -euo pipefail`, `PINNED_ZOEKT_REF="<7+ char commit SHA OR semver release tag>"` constant captured from https://github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt at WO-execution time, detect `command -v zoekt` AND `command -v zoekt-index` (print versions + exit 0 if BOTH present), otherwise print install hints: (a) Tier-1 (Go-based): `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt-index@${PINNED_ZOEKT_REF}` and `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt@${PINNED_ZOEKT_REF}` — requires `go` >= 1.21 on PATH (the install script SHOULD probe `command -v go` and print a Tier-1-prerequisites note if Go is missing); (b) Tier-2 fallback (pre-built binary): point at https://github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/releases for pre-built binaries when those exist (as of planner-time, sourcegraph/zoekt does NOT publish standalone binaries — Tier-2 may be a future ADR if Tier-1 is structurally unavailable in CI; for this WO, rely on Tier-1 only). Always exit 0 (informational only). Header comment block (10-15 lines) cites WO-0086 + P3-W10-F15 + master-plan §4.2 line 300. `chmod +x`. Pinned-immutable-ref check: `! grep -qE '"(latest|main|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh` returns empty (semver release tag OR 7+ char commit SHA acceptable).

5. EXTEND `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs` (existing file from WO-0072 — has `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` block at line ~46) by adding ONE new sync test `#[test] fn dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses()` INSIDE the existing `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` block (after `codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` at line ~91 and before the module's closing `}`). Sync `#[test]` (NOT `#[tokio::test]`) — parse-only, no async/IO. Test body mirrors `ripgrep_manifest_parses` at `tests/plugin_manifests.rs:157` verbatim in shape: (a) `let manifest = PluginManifest::from_path(repo_root().join("plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml")).expect("dependency-cruiser manifest must parse cleanly");` (executor reuses the existing `repo_root()` helper); (b) `assert_eq!(manifest.plugin.name, "dependency-cruiser", "(SA1) plugin.name must equal dependency-cruiser; observed {:?}", manifest.plugin.name);`; (c) `assert!(manifest.capabilities.provides.contains(&"architecture.dependency".to_string()), "(SA2) capabilities.provides must include architecture.dependency; observed {:?}", manifest.capabilities.provides);`; (d) `assert!(manifest.capabilities.languages.contains(&"javascript".to_string()) && manifest.capabilities.languages.contains(&"typescript".to_string()), "(SA3) capabilities.languages must include javascript and typescript; observed {:?}", manifest.capabilities.languages);`; (e) Cite DEC-0009 in a `//` comment line ABOVE the test explaining why this test does NOT call `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` (dep-cruiser is a CLI tool — `depcruise --version` does not speak JSON-RPC; the `[transport]` is a declarative metadata sentinel; the runtime path is the verify-script's `depcruise` invocation against the tmpdir-fabricated circular-dep module pair). NO `health_check` call. NO mocks. Use SA-tagged operator-readable panic messages quoting the actual observed value on failure (per WO-0042 lessons + WO-0084 §verifier `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` pattern).

6. EXTEND `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (existing file from WO-0044 + WO-0051 — has `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` block) by adding ONE new sync test `#[test] fn zoekt_manifest_parses()` INSIDE the existing `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` block (after `ripgrep_manifest_parses` at line ~157 and before the module's closing `}`). Sync `#[test]`. Test body mirrors `ripgrep_manifest_parses` verbatim in shape: (a) `let manifest = PluginManifest::from_path(repo_root().join("plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml")).expect("zoekt manifest must parse cleanly");`; (b) `assert_eq!(manifest.plugin.name, "zoekt", "(SA1) plugin.name must equal zoekt; observed {:?}", manifest.plugin.name);`; (c) `assert!(manifest.capabilities.provides.contains(&"search.text".to_string()), "(SA2) capabilities.provides must include search.text; observed {:?}", manifest.capabilities.provides);`; (d) `assert!(manifest.capabilities.languages.is_empty(), "(SA3) capabilities.languages must be empty (zoekt is language-agnostic trigram); observed {:?}", manifest.capabilities.languages);`; (e) Cite DEC-0009 in a `//` comment line ABOVE the test explaining why this test does NOT call `health_check` (Zoekt is HTTP API / external service — does not speak stdio JSON-RPC; the `[transport]` is a declarative metadata sentinel; the runtime path is the verify-script's `zoekt-index` + `zoekt` invocations against the tmpdir corpus). NO `health_check` call. NO mocks. SA-tagged panics.

7. ADDITIVELY only — do NOT modify the existing tests inside `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` (codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check) NOR inside `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` (ast_grep_manifest_health_check, probe_manifest_health_check, ripgrep_manifest_parses). Confirm via `git diff main -- crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs | grep -E '^-' | grep -vE '^---' | wc -l` returning 0 (zero deletions; only added lines + module-level rustdoc updates if needed). The leading file-level rustdoc comment blocks may be amended ONLY to update the file overview (e.g., adding `dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses` to the test list in g4_plugin_manifests.rs's leading rustdoc; adding `zoekt_manifest_parses` to plugin_manifests.rs's leading rustdoc). Per WO-0080 scope_in #8 + WO-0079 lessons §executor.

8. Both new tests live INSIDE the respective existing `mod <name> { ... }` blocks at module root (DEC-0007 frozen-selector module-root placement, carried per WO-0044 + WO-0051 + WO-0072 wrapper convention for these specific test files). Confirm: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses` resolves uniquely (substring-match without `--exact`); `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::zoekt_manifest_parses` resolves uniquely.

10. REPLACE `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` (currently TODO-stub at scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh:1-6) with a real verify script. New script structure mirroring scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh: (a) header + `set -euo pipefail`. Header comment block (15-25 lines) cites WO-0086, P3-W10-F15, master-plan §4.2 line 300, DEC-0009. (b) `REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"; cd "$REPO_ROOT"`. (c) Single-gate skip: `if [[ "${UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E:-0}" != "0" ]]; then echo '[SKIP] P3-W10-F15: search plugin smoke skipped via UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E'; exit 0; fi` (NEW env-var for the search-group plugin smokes; analogous to UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E and UCIL_SKIP_QUALITY_PLUGIN_E2E — confirm in scope by grep-checking that `UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E` does not yet exist anywhere in the repo via `! grep -rE 'UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E' . --exclude-dir=target --exclude-dir=node_modules` returning empty BEFORE this WO introduces it; document the new env-var in the verify script's header comment AND the manifest's header comment). (d) Assert zoekt + zoekt-index on PATH: `if ! command -v zoekt >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "[FAIL] P3-W10-F15: zoekt not on PATH; see scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh" >&2; exit 1; fi` AND analogous for zoekt-index. Print `zoekt --version` AND `zoekt-index --version` (first lines) for the verifier log. Also assert ripgrep on PATH (the comparison baseline): `command -v rg`. (e) Assert manifest exists: `test -f plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml`. (f) Run cargo test: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::zoekt_manifest_parses 2>&1 | tee "$CARGO_LOG" >/dev/null` then assert `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests? passed' "$CARGO_LOG"`. (g) Trigram-index + warm-query smoke: `TMPDIR=$(mktemp -d)`, `trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT`. Copy multiple fixture projects to the tmpdir to build a non-trivial corpus: `mkdir "$TMPDIR/corpus"; cp -r tests/fixtures/rust-project tests/fixtures/typescript-project tests/fixtures/python-project "$TMPDIR/corpus/"`. Build the Zoekt index: `INDEX_DIR="$TMPDIR/zoekt-index"; mkdir "$INDEX_DIR"; zoekt-index -index "$INDEX_DIR" "$TMPDIR/corpus" 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0086-zoekt-index.log`. (h) Run identical query through both Zoekt (warm — execute once for warm-up, ignore wall-clock; then execute and time) and ripgrep, capture wall-clocks: pick a query that appears in multiple fixture files (e.g., `"fn evaluate"` appears in rust-project; `"export"` is broad but works; OR the executor selects an SA-tagged query from one of the fixture files). For Zoekt warm-up + measure: `zoekt -index_dir "$INDEX_DIR" 'fn evaluate' >/dev/null 2>&1; ZOEKT_T_NS=$(date +%s%N); zoekt -index_dir "$INDEX_DIR" 'fn evaluate' > /tmp/wo-0086-zoekt-out.txt 2>&1; ZOEKT_NS=$(($(date +%s%N) - ZOEKT_T_NS))`. For ripgrep: `RG_T_NS=$(date +%s%N); rg --files-with-matches 'fn evaluate' "$TMPDIR/corpus" > /tmp/wo-0086-rg-out.txt 2>&1; RG_NS=$(($(date +%s%N) - RG_T_NS))`. (i) Print both wall-clocks for the verifier log: `echo "[INFO] zoekt warm query: ${ZOEKT_NS} ns; ripgrep query: ${RG_NS} ns"`. (j) Correctness invariant (LOAD-BEARING): the matched-path SET (NOT order) returned by Zoekt MUST be a non-empty subset of the matched-path set returned by ripgrep (Zoekt may have indexer-side filters, but every Zoekt-reported file MUST also be ripgrep-reported). Implement via `sort -u`: extract path components from both outputs (Zoekt's output format is `<file>:<line>:<text>` with optional ANSI codes; strip ANSI via `sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*m//g'` then `awk -F':' '{print $1}' | sort -u`; ripgrep's --files-with-matches outputs path-per-line directly, sort -u). Use `comm -23 <(zoekt_paths) <(rg_paths)` returning empty (every Zoekt-reported path is also ripgrep-reported); AND `[ $(zoekt_paths | wc -l) -gt 0 ]` (non-empty match set; proves Zoekt actually found results). (k) Wall-clock comparison (SOFT — tolerates small-corpus noise): `if [ $ZOEKT_NS -gt $((RG_NS + 50_000_000)) ]; then echo "[FAIL] P3-W10-F15: zoekt warm query (${ZOEKT_NS} ns) is more than 50ms slower than ripgrep (${RG_NS} ns) — master-plan §4.2 'faster than ripgrep on warm index' regression" >&2; exit 1; fi` (50ms tolerance absorbs small-corpus noise; on representative corpora Zoekt's warm-index wins comfortably; on tiny fixtures both are <10ms and the comparison trivially passes). (l) On all-green print `[OK] P3-W10-F15` exit 0. `chmod +x`.

11. **Pre-bake 4 mutation entries — 2 per feature.** Pattern carried from WO-0042 → WO-0085 (now 41+ WOs deep). Each mutation: in-place sed Edit + targeted cargo-test or verify-script selector + `git checkout --` restore + md5sum verify. M1 (F14, manifest provides corruption): `sed -i 's|provides = \["architecture.dependency"\]|provides = []|' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` → `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses` MUST FAIL with the SA2 panic (`(SA2) capabilities.provides must include architecture.dependency; observed []`). Restore: `git checkout -- plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml`. M2 (F14, plugin name corruption): `sed -i 's|^name = "dependency-cruiser"|name = "x"|' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` → `cargo test ...` MUST FAIL with the SA1 panic (`(SA1) plugin.name must equal dependency-cruiser; observed "x"`). Restore. M3 (F15, manifest languages corruption): `sed -i 's|languages = \[\]|languages = ["only-rust"]|' plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` → `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::zoekt_manifest_parses` MUST FAIL with the SA3 panic (`(SA3) capabilities.languages must be empty (zoekt is language-agnostic trigram); observed ["only-rust"]`). Restore. M4 (F15, verify-script wall-clock comparison erasure): `sed -i 's|if \[ \$ZOEKT_NS -gt|# DELETED if [ $ZOEKT_NS -gt|' scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` (defangs the wall-clock guard line — semantic intent is to verify M4 catches a regression where the load-bearing wall-clock comparison is silently disabled; if the executor chooses a different wall-clock-guard form, M4 adapts to that form and the executor pre-stages md5sum). Restore. Each mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, ≤ 60 seconds wall-time. Pre-stage md5 snapshots: `md5sum plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0086-depcruiser-orig.md5`, `md5sum plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml > /tmp/wo-0086-zoekt-orig.md5`, `md5sum scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh > /tmp/wo-0086-zoekt-verify-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation. Confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0086-<name>-orig.md5` returning OK after each `git checkout --`.

12. Pre-flight word-ban grep MUST cover ONLY production-side files (manifests, install scripts, verify scripts) and EXEMPT integration-test files under `tests/`. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` returns empty before first push. The integration-test files' rustdocs may mention 'Mocking ... is forbidden' inside a forbidden-pattern declaration (per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 carve-out).

13. Pinned-immutable-ref policy: confirm `! grep -qE '"(latest|next|main|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` returns empty (the floating-ref class). Frozen semver release tags (e.g., `@1.2.3`) AND 7+ char commit SHAs (Zoekt) are acceptable; bare branch refs are forbidden. Per master-plan §13 + WO-0069 lesson §executor: floating refs make verifier reruns non-deterministic.

14. The integration-test files MUST use `tokio::process::Command` (not `std::process::Command`) IF any helper subprocess invocations are introduced by this WO — per WO-0075 §executor lesson W1 + WO-0080 pre-emptive carve-out. The two new tests are PARSE-ONLY (no subprocess invocation, no async/IO — just `PluginManifest::from_path` + asserts) so this rule applies vacuously here, but pre-flight `! grep -qE 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs` (covers all five tests across both files) before first push, regression-guarding any future subprocess additions.

15. `unsafe std::env::set_var` IS FORBIDDEN in this WO's test code. Both dep-cruiser and Zoekt are external CLI tools resolved via PATH; no parent-process env-var mutation is needed (contrast with WO-0076 Semgrep `unsafe set_var("SEMGREP_PATH", ...)` which was the W1+W2 debt — this WO sidesteps it entirely by virtue of standard PATH-resolution). NEW unsafe blocks must NOT be introduced by this WO. Per WO-0076 §verifier lesson 'unsafe block without ADR cite — soft critic-warning category'.

16. DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out applies to TWO small augmentations in TWO different test files — the test additions to g4_plugin_manifests.rs (~70 LOC for dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses) and to plugin_manifests.rs (~70 LOC for zoekt_manifest_parses) are cohesive units PER FILE and should be separately committed (each test alongside the manifest it validates). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0079 commit `c448dfc` precedent (graphiti g3 augmentation) in each commit body. Do NOT split the test-additions into multiple commits per file — one commit per (manifest + test + install-script + verify-script) coherent unit per feature.

17. §15.2 tracing spans do NOT apply to plugin-manifest landings — these features add data files (TOML manifests) + integration tests + helper scripts, NOT new orchestration code. Do NOT introduce new `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` annotations under this WO. Cite WO-0067 lessons §planner #2 ('pure-deterministic / data-only modules: §15.2 tracing does NOT apply') as precedent.

18. WO-0069 lessons §planner #2 carve-out (pre-emptive API-key-gate for plugins requiring keys) does NOT apply here — neither dep-cruiser nor Zoekt requires an API key. The verify scripts' gates are single-axis: UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E for F14 (extends existing); UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E for F15 (NEW env-var documented in scope_in #10).

19. WO-0069 lessons §planner #3 carve-out (eventual-consistency OR-disjunction): both dep-cruiser and Zoekt's tool-level smokes are fully synchronous — `depcruise --output-type json` returns deterministic structured JSON on first invocation; `zoekt -index_dir` returns deterministic match list once the index is built. AND-conjunctive assertions are fine. No OR-disjunction needed.

20. AC25 reworded per WO-0070 lessons §planner #4: 'feat branch carries zero merge commits' (using `git log feat/WO-0086-deps-cruiser-and-zoekt-manifests ^main --merges` returning empty), NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form. Same spirit, more precise wording.

21. Carry-forward standing protocol (41+ WOs deep, WO-0042..WO-0085): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement; the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-daemon 85 75` reporting `line=0%` is the known sccache interaction. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-daemon` lines in phase-3.sh output per AC23 standing protocol.

22. Carry-forward standing protocol: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations). Executor runs constituent cargo-test + clippy + smoke sub-checks in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Pre-existing standing scope_out item.

23. Verifier MUST NOT set `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS`, `UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E`, OR `UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E` envs (per WO-0079 scope_in #25 precedent extended to this WO and the new search-group env-var introduced here).

24. RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include the M1/M2/M3/M4 mutation contract section with: (a) named file path, (b) the exact patch (e.g., `s/provides = \["architecture.dependency"\]/provides = []/`), (c) the targeted cargo-test or verify-script selector, (d) the expected SA-tagged panic line OR exit-1 reason, (e) the restore command (`git checkout -- <path>`), (f) the pre-mutation md5 snapshot path (`/tmp/wo-0086-<file>-orig.md5`). Mirror the WO-0067/0068/0070/0072/0080/0085 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

25. Pre-flight `which depcruise` AND `which zoekt zoekt-index rg` AND `which jq` before first push — the verify scripts will spawn these from PATH; missing tools produce false-positive failure modes that mask M1-M4 detection. Per WO-0069 lessons §verifier #2 + WO-0076/0077/0079/0080 carry. If `depcruise` OR `zoekt`/`zoekt-index` are missing on the executor's machine, the executor may install them via `npm install -g dependency-cruiser@<VERSION>` AND `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt-index@<REF>` + `go install github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/cmd/zoekt@<REF>` BEFORE the first push.

26. WO-0044 + WO-0051 + WO-0072 are the canonical references for the parse-only manifest test pattern. F14 mirrors `ripgrep_manifest_parses` (parse-only) with the architecture group's UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E gate. F15 mirrors `ripgrep_manifest_parses` directly in the same test file. ONE feature, ONE manifest, ONE install script, ONE replacement verify script, ONE additive parse-only test PER FEATURE. The TWO features are bundled because both are CLI/external-service tools without MCP transport; both share the WO-0051 declarative-sentinel precedent under DEC-0009.

27. F14 `attempts=0` AND F15 `attempts=0` (verified at planner-time via `jq '[.features[] | select(.id=="P3-W10-F14" or .id=="P3-W10-F15") | {id, attempts}]' ucil-build/feature-list.json`). This WO is the **first attempt for both**; clean execution expected per the 41-WO-deep mature template.

28. dependency-cruiser version pin SHOULD be the latest stable semver from `curl -sL https://registry.npmjs.org/dependency-cruiser | jq -r '."dist-tags".latest'` AT WO-EXECUTION TIME (NOT cached at planning time). Zoekt has no canonical pinned semver release; pin to a 7+ char commit SHA from https://github.com/sourcegraph/zoekt/commits/main HEAD AT WO-EXECUTION TIME (or any recent stable commit). Document both pinned refs in each manifest's `pinned <DATE>` header comment block AND each install script's `PINNED_<TOOL>_VERSION` constant.

29. Bundle-shape rationale (cite in commit body for the first feat commit): both F14 and F15 follow the WO-0051 ripgrep parse-only-manifest precedent because neither tool speaks stdio JSON-RPC (dep-cruiser is a CLI invocation; Zoekt is HTTP API / external service). The two features bundle naturally because they share the SAME manifest+install+verify+parse-only-test template scaled to two plugins, mirroring WO-0044's ast-grep+probe pair shape (two tools, two manifests, two install scripts, two health-check tests in the same test file). Differences: F14 lives in `plugins/architecture/` and extends `g4_plugin_manifests.rs`; F15 lives in `plugins/search/` and extends `plugin_manifests.rs` — two existing test files extended in parallel.

30. The verify scripts' wall-clock instrumentation (Zoekt vs ripgrep) uses `date +%s%N` (nanosecond-resolution since-epoch) for portable timing without depending on `bc` or arithmetic helpers beyond bash builtins. The 50ms tolerance (`50_000_000` ns) is documented in scope_in #10 (k) AND inline in the verify script with a comment citing master-plan §4.2 'faster than ripgrep on warm index' AND the small-corpus-noise rationale. If the executor finds the timing comparison is consistently noisy on the build machine (Zoekt slower than ripgrep + 50ms on the fixture-scale corpus), the executor may relax the tolerance to 200ms and document the relaxation in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section per WO-0070/0083/0084 spirit-over-literal precedent.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT implement a `DependencyCruiserG4Source` adapter for dep-cruiser NOR a `ZoektG2Source` adapter for Zoekt (both deferred to follow-up WOs that wire the daemon-side runtime against the G2/G4 executors when those production-wirings ship — same shape as the G3/G4/G5/G6/G7/G8 production-wiring follow-ups already deferred per WO-0084/0085 §technical-debt).

2. Do NOT modify ANY existing manifest (plugins/architecture/codegraphcontext/, plugins/search/probe/, plugins/search/ripgrep/, plugins/structural/{ast-grep,scip,serena}/, plugins/quality/{eslint,ruff,semgrep}/, plugins/knowledge/{codebase-memory,graphiti,mem0}/, plugins/context/{context7,repomix}/, plugins/platform/{filesystem,git,github}/, plugins/testing/{mcp-pytest-runner,test-runner}/) — frozen per their respective landing WOs; this WO only ADDS plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/ AND plugins/search/zoekt/.

3. Do NOT modify the existing tests inside `mod g4_plugin_manifests { ... }` (codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check) NOR inside `mod plugin_manifests { ... }` (ast_grep_manifest_health_check, probe_manifest_health_check, ripgrep_manifest_parses) — only ADD the new parse-only tests. The leading file-level rustdoc may be amended ONLY to update each file's overview (e.g., add dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses to g4_plugin_manifests.rs's leading rustdoc; add zoekt_manifest_parses to plugin_manifests.rs's leading rustdoc) per scope_in #7.

4. Do NOT add new G2 or G4 plugin manifests beyond dep-cruiser + Zoekt under this WO (deptry, skott, Nx, Bazel MCP, GitNexus, Axon, codedb are all future-WO scope per master-plan §4.2 + §4.4 + §14.1).

5. Do NOT wire the new manifests into any `PluginManager::initialize_all` / startup-discovery path beyond what already exists — the WO-0044 + WO-0072 manifests are loaded on-demand by the integration tests via `PluginManifest::from_path(&manifest_path)`, and the new manifests should be loadable the same way without daemon-startup changes.
6. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider or embedding-provider configuration (neither tool has an LLM dependency).

7. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning beyond the existing repository state — Zoekt's HTTP-API webserver is OUT OF SCOPE for this WO (the verify script invokes `zoekt-index` to build a local index + `zoekt` CLI to query it; NO `zoekt-webserver` daemon is started). Future ADR may revisit if a daemon-mode integration is needed.
8. Do NOT introduce any cross-WO dependency wiring (downstream WOs consume F14 + F15; that's their scope).
9. Do NOT add any new `tracing::instrument` annotations to source code (per scope_in #17).
10. Do NOT add or modify entries in `ucil-build/feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.

11. Do NOT modify any file under `tests/fixtures/**` — verify scripts are read-only against the fixture (per WO-0080 forbidden_paths). The deliberate-circular-dep `cycle-a.ts` + `cycle-b.ts` fabrication for F14 lands in the `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY (per scope_in #9 (g)). The Zoekt index storage for F15 lives in `mktemp -d` tmpdir ONLY (per scope_in #10 (g)).
12. Do NOT alter `scripts/gate/**` or `scripts/flip-feature.sh` (forbidden_paths).

13. Do NOT introduce new env-vars beyond UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E (which is the FIRST G2-search-group env-var; UCIL_SKIP_ARCHITECTURE_PLUGIN_E2E already exists from WO-0072 verbatim and IS NOT a new env-var). Document UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E in scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh's header comment block AND plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml's header comment block. Single env-var per group; F15 establishes the search-group convention.
14. Do NOT introduce `unsafe std::env::set_var` blocks (WO-0076 W1+W2 debt). Both tools resolve via standard PATH; no parent-process env-var mutation needed (per scope_in #15).
15. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (41+ WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.

16. Do NOT split the F14 OR F15 deliverables across multiple feat commits beyond the per-feature module-coherence carve-out — each feature's manifest + install script + verify script + parse-only test is ONE coherent unit per DEC-0005 (~150-200 LOC each commit).

17. Do NOT introduce a `Tier-2 fallback` for either tool unless the executor encounters a structural blocker on Tier-1 — Tier-1 (npm install for dep-cruiser; go install for Zoekt) IS the canonical install path per master-plan §4.2/§4.4 + the WO-0051 ripgrep precedent (no Tier-2 needed since the tools are independently maintained as system binaries). If Tier-1 fails for either tool, executor MAY escalate via ADR before falling back.

18. Do NOT fabricate a circular dependency in the `tests/fixtures/typescript-project/` source tree — the fixture is read-only per forbidden_paths; the cycle-a/cycle-b fabrication MUST be in the `mktemp -d` tmpdir copy ONLY (per scope_in #9 (g) + scope_out #11). Verified at planner-time the fixture itself has NO cycle (clean DAG: types.ts ← {filter-engine, repository, task-manager}.ts; task-manager imports repository AND filter-engine, but no back-edge to task-manager).

19. Do NOT introduce a new `tests/integration/test_<name>.rs` file — both new tests live INSIDE existing peer test files (g4_plugin_manifests.rs and plugin_manifests.rs) per the WO-0044/0051/0072 module-root wrapper convention.

20. Do NOT pin Zoekt to a moving branch ref (`@main`, `@master`, `@HEAD`); pin to a specific 7+ char commit SHA OR a semver release tag if upstream cuts one (sourcegraph/zoekt does not publish formal semver releases as of planner-time, so commit-SHA pinning is the canonical form per master-plan §13).
acceptance: AC01 — `test -f plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` (manifest exists at the canonical path).
AC02 — `test -f plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` (manifest exists at the canonical path).
AC03 — `test -x scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh` AND `test -x scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` (both verify scripts executable).
AC04 — `test -x scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh` AND `test -x scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh` (both install hints executable).
AC05 — `grep -q '^name = "dependency-cruiser"' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` AND `grep -q '^name = "zoekt"' plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml`.

AC06 — F14 capability declaration: `grep -q '"architecture.dependency"' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml` AND `grep -qE 'languages = \[.*"javascript".*"typescript".*\]|languages = \[.*"typescript".*"javascript".*\]' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml`.
AC07 — F15 capability declaration: `grep -q '"search.text"' plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` AND `grep -q 'languages = \[\]' plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` (Zoekt is language-agnostic).
AC08 — `! grep -qE '"(latest|next|main|master|head|develop|dev|nightly)"' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` (no moving refs).

AC09 — `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` (production-side word-ban).
AC10 — F14 cargo test: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::dependency_cruiser_manifest_parses 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. 1 passed'`.
AC11 — F15 cargo test: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::zoekt_manifest_parses 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. 1 passed'`.
AC12 — `cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings` exits 0 AND `cargo fmt --check -p ucil-daemon` exits 0.
AC13 — Workspace-wide regression: `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` exits 0 (no cross-crate test regressed by the new manifests).

AC14 — F14 verify script: `bash scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh` exits 0 on a system with `depcruise` on PATH AND prints `[OK] P3-W10-F14`. The script's circular-dep smoke against the tmpdir-fabricated cycle-a/cycle-b pair surfaces at least one circular violation in dep-cruiser's JSON output.

AC15 — F15 verify script: `bash scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh` exits 0 on a system with `zoekt`+`zoekt-index`+`rg` on PATH AND prints `[OK] P3-W10-F15`. The script's correctness invariant (Zoekt match-set ⊆ ripgrep match-set) holds AND the wall-clock guard (`zoekt_ns ≤ rg_ns + 50_000_000`) holds within the 50ms tolerance.

AC16 — Regression sentinels: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test g4_plugin_manifests g4_plugin_manifests::codegraphcontext_manifest_health_check` exits 0 AND `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check plugin_manifests::ripgrep_manifest_parses` exits 0 (the existing per-test selectors stay green; adding the new parse-only tests does not regress any existing test).
AC17 — M1 mutation (F14 manifest provides corruption) FAILS the F14 cargo-test selector with the SA2 panic body.
AC18 — M2 mutation (F14 plugin name corruption) FAILS the F14 cargo-test selector with the SA1 panic body.
AC19 — M3 mutation (F15 manifest languages corruption) FAILS the F15 cargo-test selector with the SA3 panic body.

AC20 — M4 mutation (F15 verify-script wall-clock guard erasure) FAILS the F15 verify script (defanged guard means a slow Zoekt would silently pass when it should fail; M4 verifies the guard line is load-bearing — restoration recovers correctness; if executor selects an alternate M4 form that achieves the same regression detection, document the form in the RFR's Mutation contract section).
AC21 — All four mutations are reversible via `git checkout -- <file>`; md5sum match restored after each (tracked via /tmp/wo-0086-<name>-orig.md5 snapshots).

AC22 — RFR file ucil-build/work-orders/0086-ready-for-review.md exists with: (a) summary, (b) M1/M2/M3/M4 mutation contracts in the canonical shape, (c) live `depcruise --version` + `zoekt --version` + `rg --version` capture transcript with timestamps, (d) bundle-shape rationale citing WO-0051 + WO-0044 precedents, (e) UCIL_SKIP_SEARCH_PLUGIN_E2E new env-var rationale, (f) any disclosed deviations from scope_in (per WO-0070/0083/0084 spirit-over-literal pattern).
AC23 — Feat branch carries zero merge commits: `git log feat/WO-0086-deps-cruiser-and-zoekt-manifests ^main --merges` returns empty (per scope_in #20 + WO-0070 §planner #4).
AC24 — `tests/fixtures/**` is NOT modified: `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'tests/fixtures/**'` returns empty.

AC25 — `ucil-build/feature-list.json` and `feature-list.schema.json` are NOT modified: `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'ucil-build/feature-list.json' 'ucil-build/feature-list.schema.json'` returns empty.
AC26 — `ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md` is NOT modified: `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- 'ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md'` returns empty.

AC27 — Stub-scan: `rg -nE 'todo!\(\)|unimplemented!\(\)|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError|TODO: implement' crates/ucil-daemon/tests/g4_plugin_manifests.rs crates/ucil-daemon/tests/plugin_manifests.rs scripts/verify/P3-W10-F14.sh scripts/verify/P3-W10-F15.sh scripts/devtools/install-dependency-cruiser.sh scripts/devtools/install-zoekt.sh plugins/architecture/dependency-cruiser/plugin.toml plugins/search/zoekt/plugin.toml` returns ZERO hits in the new/edited diff regions (the existing untouched ast-grep/probe/ripgrep/codegraphcontext blocks of the test files are out of scope for this scan; only the new test regions matter).

AC28 — Commit cadence: 4-6 commits matching DEC-0005 module-coherence (per-feature feat commit + per-feature test/verify commit + final RFR commit; F14 and F15 deliverables in separate coherent commits).
